title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people – TFN application
description: How Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people can apply for a tax file number (TFN).
keywords: aboriginal, torres strait, torres strait islander, NAT1589, NAT 1589, TFN application, aboriginal TFN, torres strait TFN, tax file number, apply TFN

content_blocks.text: Eligibility to apply for a TFN
Australian residents can apply for a TFN at any age. It is free to apply for a TFN.
How you apply (online or by post) will depend on if you can use online services and the identity documents you have.

items: ATO online services using a Digital ID, such as myID External Link . You need to be an Australian citizen be at least 15 years old have a Strong myID External Link identity strength.

Australia Post . After you complete the online form you will need to print the form and take it when you go to an interview at a participating Australia Post retail outlet External Link show your current original identity documents at the interview.

content_blocks.text: If you can't apply online you will need to apply by post.
You need to complete the paper form Tax file number – application or enquiry for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people (NAT 1589).
To get a copy of the form, you can:

items: order it online through the ATO Publication Ordering Service External Link

content_blocks.text: Proof of identity for the paper form

If you can't provide enough documents that prove your identity, you will need to ask a referee who has known you for 12 months or more to complete the Proof of identity reference on page 7 of the form.
This must be an authorised referee. Examples include:

items: an authorised government officer, such as a police officer Services Australia agent nurse.

content_blocks.text: The referee must not be a family member.
You can lodge this form be either:

items: posting it to the address on page 6 of the form
going to a Services Australia centre External Link , if you're a Services Australia customer.

content_text:
## Eligibility to apply for a TFN
Australian residents can apply for a TFN at any age. It is free to apply for a TFN.
## How to apply
How you apply (online or by post) will depend on if you can use online services and the identity documents you have.
### Apply online
You can apply online through:
- ATO online services using a Digital ID, such as myID External Link . You need to be an Australian citizen be at least 15 years old have a Strong myID External Link identity strength.
- Australia Post . After you complete the online form you will need to print the form and take it when you go to an interview at a participating Australia Post retail outlet External Link show your current original identity documents at the interview.
If you can't apply online you will need to apply by post.
### Apply by post
You need to complete the paper form Tax file number – application or enquiry for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people (NAT 1589).
To get a copy of the form, you can:
- order it online through the ATO Publication Ordering Service External Link
- phone the Indigenous helpline on 13 10 30 .
#### Proof of identity for the paper form
If you can't provide enough documents that prove your identity, you will need to ask a referee who has known you for 12 months or more to complete the Proof of identity reference on page 7 of the form.
This must be an authorised referee. Examples include:
- a council chairperson
- a community manager
- a school principal
- a minister of religion
- a doctor
- a senior nursing sister
- an authorised government officer, such as a police officer Services Australia agent nurse.
The referee must not be a family member.
You can lodge this form be either:
- posting it to the address on page 6 of the form
- going to a Services Australia centre External Link , if you're a Services Australia customer.
